text: We present a high-performance AI/TP system that on the occasion of the 50th anniversary of the Mizar project can automatically prove about 60% of the Mizar theorems in a hammer mode. This rate rises to 75% if the ATPs are limited to using only the statements in the premises of the human-made proofs. We explain our techniques and provide information on the resources needed for our large-scale experiments. These include the E and Vampire theorem provers, each enhanced with two neural components, namely ENIGMA and Deepire. The proofs in the training corpora that amount to millions are produced within an incremental learning loop. We present several representative examples of relatively difficult Mizar theorems that can now be proved fully automatically.
explanation: The text reads like a plausible technical abstract with domain-specific terms (hammer mode, ATPs, E and Vampire provers) and realistic performance figures; there are no obvious AI-typical repetitions or generic filler, though a couple of names (Deepire) could be fictional.
